title: Why Tiny Colleges Might Be the Future of Education
subtitle: 小而美的大学：教育的另一种可能

paragraphs.en: We're experiencing the rebirth of smallness. Farmers markets, tiny homes, and brew pubs all exemplify our love of smallness. So do charter schools, coffee shops, and local bookstores. Small is often more affordable, healthier, and sustainable, but its finest characteristic, the one that turns charm into love, is that going small allows us to be more fully who we are.
paragraphs.zh: 我们正在经历小规模的复兴。农贸市场、迷你住宅和精酿酒吧都体现了我们对小巧事物的热爱。特许学校、咖啡馆和本地书店亦是如此。小规模往往更实惠、更健康、更可持续，但它最美好的特质——将魅力转化为热爱的那一点——在于小规模让我们能更完整地做自己。

paragraphs.en: In higher education the trend is mostly in the opposite direction: universities with 20,000 or 30,000 students are considered mid-sized. The nation's largest university, Arizona State University, has 80,000 students on campus and aims to enroll another 100,000 students online. At the other end of the spectrum is a handful of colleges that have fewer than a hundred students on campus and no online courses: colleges such as Sterling College, Thomas More College of Liberal Arts, and Deep Springs College. These colleges are so small that they can only be called tiny.
paragraphs.zh: 高等教育领域的趋势则大多相反：拥有两三万名学生的大学被视为“中型”。美国最大的大学亚利桑那州立大学有八万在校生，并计划在线招收另外十万名学生。光谱的另一端则是少数在校生不足百人且无在线课程的高校：如斯特林学院、托马斯·莫尔文理学院和深泉学院。这些学院规模如此之小，只能称之为“微型”。

paragraphs.en: Tiny colleges focus not just on a young person's intellect, but on the young person as a whole. Equally important, tiny colleges ask, 'How can education contribute to human flourishing and the well-being of the world?' And they shape a college experience to address that question. They replace concerns about institutional growth with attention to the growth of students as fully developed participants in their communities.
paragraphs.zh: 微型学院不仅关注年轻人的智力，更关注年轻人的整体发展。同样重要的是，它们追问：“教育如何促进人类繁荣与世界福祉？”并围绕这一问题塑造大学经历。它们不再执着于机构扩张，转而关注学生作为社区中全面发展参与者的成长。

paragraphs.en: I've had the privilege of teaching at three different institutions of higher learning during my career — a small liberal arts college and two mid-sized public universities. I've also been profoundly disappointed in each of these institutions when it comes to helping students prepare for the many responsibilities of adulthood. Administrators focus on the business of running a university, and most faculty focus on their scholarship and teaching their discipline. Little deliberate attention is given to how students mature as individuals and social beings.
paragraphs.zh: 我职业生涯中有幸在三所不同高校任教——一所小型文理学院和两所中型公立大学。但我也对这些机构在帮助学生为成年后的诸多责任做好准备方面深感失望。行政人员专注于运营大学的业务，大多数教师则专注于学术研究和学科教学。很少有人刻意关注学生如何作为个体和社会人走向成熟。

paragraphs.en: Having just retired from teaching at a public university, I'm now returning to my hometown of Flagstaff, Arizona, to establish a tiny college — Flagstaff College. I'm convinced there's a need for another type of education, one devoted to helping students come into their own and into this beautiful and troubled world. Young people need an education that will provide them with meaning, hope, courage, and passion, as well as information and skills. Large institutions, I believe, are particularly ill-suited to this type of education.
paragraphs.zh: 我刚从公立大学退休，如今回到家乡亚利桑那州弗拉格斯塔夫，创办一所微型学院——弗拉格斯塔夫学院。我深信需要另一种教育，一种致力于帮助学生找到自我并融入这个美丽而纷扰的世界的教育。年轻人需要一种能赋予他们意义、希望、勇气和热情，同时传授知识和技能的教育。我相信大型机构尤其不适合这种教育。

words.meaning: adj. 不合适的，不适合的

paragraphs.en: There's no 'best of' list when it comes to tiny colleges, at least not yet. But around the country people are creating new colleges that provide an alternative to small liberal arts colleges, large public universities, and online education. With only 26 students, Deep Springs is the smallest college in the country and, quite likely, the most atypical. Located on a working cattle ranch on the California-Nevada border, Deep Springs is a private, residential, two-year college for men, committed to educating students for a life of service to humanity.
paragraphs.zh: 微型学院还没有“最佳”榜单，至少目前还没有。但全国各地都有人在创建新型学院，为小型文理学院、大型公立大学和在线教育提供替代方案。仅有26名学生的深泉学院是全美最小的学院，也很可能是最非典型的。它坐落于加州与内华达州交界处一个运营中的牧场上，是一所私立寄宿制两年制男子学院，致力于培养学生为人类服务的人生。

words.meaning: adj. 非典型的，不寻常的

paragraphs.en: Founded by the electricity tycoon Nunn in 1917, Deep Springs' curriculum revolves around academics, labor, and self-governance. In addition to their courses, students are charged with running the 155-acre ranch and overseeing the functioning of the college. Students chair both the admissions and the curriculum committees. 'Living in close community with one's teachers and fellow students, and being forced to take on adult responsibilities, makes for one's growth as a person,' says William Hunt, who graduated last year.
paragraphs.zh: 深泉学院由电力大亨纳恩于1917年创办，其“课程”围绕学术、劳动和自治展开。除了课程学习，学生还需负责经营155英亩的牧场并监督学院运作。学生担任招生委员会和课程委员会的主席。去年毕业的威廉·亨特说：“与老师和同学生活在紧密的社群中，被迫承担成人责任，能促进个人成长。要在那样的社群中长期存在，你必须超越自己是否足够有才华或有原则的疑问，转而思考如何拓展自己和同伴，如何与他们一起学习更多。”

paragraphs.en: Sterling College, in Craftsbury Common, Vermont, is also very small — fewer than 100 students. Unlike Deep Springs, Sterling focuses its curriculum on environmental and social justice issues, but like Deep Springs it places a high value on personal responsibility and manual labor. According to its catalog, a college education at Sterling combines rigorous academics, roll-up-your-sleeves challenges, and good old hard work. The average tuition at a small liberal-arts college is $30,000 to $40,000 a year, not including living costs, compared to $8,000 to $10,000 a year for tuition alone at a public university.
paragraphs.zh: 佛蒙特州克拉夫茨伯里康芒的斯特林学院也非常小——学生不足百人。与深泉不同，斯特林的课程聚焦环境与社会正义议题，但同样高度重视个人责任和体力劳动。根据其课程目录，斯特林的教育融合了严谨的学术、挽起袖子干的挑战和踏踏实实的辛勤工作。小型文理学院年均学费在三万至四万美元之间，不含生活费，而公立大学仅学费每年就八千至一万美元。

paragraphs.en: Of the tiny colleges, only Deep Springs doesn't charge tuition or room and board; students pay only for books and travel costs. If tiny schools are to become a player on the higher education scene, they will need to find a way to be truly affordable. Doing so may not be that difficult so long as they do not pattern themselves too closely on existing norms. We've come to believe that a good college should have many academic programs, excellent facilities, posh dorms, an array of athletic programs, and a world-class student activity center. Imagine a good college without a climbing wall!
paragraphs.zh: 在这些微型学院中，只有深泉不收学费和食宿费，学生仅需支付书本费和往返交通费。如果微型学校想在高等教育领域占有一席之地，就必须找到真正可负担的方式。只要不过度模仿现有规范，做到这一点或许并不难。我们已习惯于认为一所好大学应有众多学术项目、一流设施、豪华宿舍、各种体育项目以及世界级的学生活动中心。想象一所没有攀岩墙的好大学！

paragraphs.en: We also have accepted that college presidents and their many vice-presidents should be paid like their counterparts in the business world, and that higher education requires an elaborate technology infrastructure. All of this drives up the cost of education. The trick to making tiny colleges affordable is simplicity. At its core, education is a human-to-human interaction. Reflecting on his own education, President Garfield once commented that an ideal college would consist of nothing more than the legendary teacher Mark Hopkins on one end of a log and a student on the other.
paragraphs.zh: 我们还接受了大学校长及其众多副校长的薪酬应与商界同行看齐，以及高等教育需要精密的技术基础设施。这一切都推高了教育成本。让微型学院可负担的“秘诀”在于简单。教育的核心是人与人的互动。加菲尔德总统在反思自身教育时曾评论，理想的大学不过是传奇教师马克·霍普金斯坐在圆木一头，另一头坐着一名学生。

paragraphs.en: The economics of a tiny college, in other words, might be similar to that of a tiny house. Because it is small, a tiny house costs less to build and less to furnish, insure, and maintain. But the economic benefits don't end there. Tiny homes discourage homeowners from buying stuff they really don't need, because there's no place to put it. I'm a late convert to the idea of tiny colleges, and I fully understand the need for many diverse types of educational institutions. Academic research and job training are important, but tiny colleges aren't suited for either.
paragraphs.zh: 换句话说，微型学院的经济学可能类似于微型住宅的经济学。因为小，微型住宅建造成本更低，装修、保险和维护费用也更少。但经济效益不止于此。微型住宅会抑制房主购买他们并不真正需要的东西，因为没地方放。我是微型学院理念的晚期皈依者，也完全理解需要多种不同类型教育机构。学术研究和职业培训很重要，但微型学院两者都不擅长。

words.meaning: n. 皈依者，改变信仰者

paragraphs.en: The educational needs of a complex society are themselves complex, and no single model can meet all of these needs. But I'm now convinced there's an educational need that's going almost completely unmet: the need to help young people transition into adulthood. Tiny colleges can do this better than any other type of educational institution. The ultimate justification for a tiny college is the conviction that each of us comes into our full humanity by close interaction with those who know and care for us, and that one of the basic purposes of higher education is social.
paragraphs.zh: 复杂社会的教育需求本身就是复杂的，没有单一模式能满足所有需求。但我现在确信有一种教育需求几乎完全未被满足：帮助年轻人过渡到成年的需要。微型学院在这方面能比任何其他类型的教育机构做得更好。微型学院的终极理由在于这样一种信念：我们每个人都通过与了解和关心我们的人的密切互动而获得完整的人性，而高等教育的基本目的之一是社会性的。

paragraphs.en: Although we give lip service to the idea that a college education will make us better people, when all's said and done, we think of higher education primarily in economic terms. We've come to think of higher education as a means to make a living rather than make a life. We've also come to see it as a private good rather than a public one. Tiny colleges are not the answer to all our educational requirements, but they're an answer to one of our most basic necessities: the need to produce thoughtful, engaged, and compassionate human beings.
paragraphs.zh: 尽管我们口头上承认大学教育能让我们成为更好的人，但归根结底，我们主要从经济角度看待高等教育。我们已将其视为谋生的手段，而非创造生活的方式。我们还将其视为私人产品而非公共产品。微型学院并非解决所有教育需求的答案，但它回应了我们最基本的需求之一：培养有思想、有担当、有同情心的人。

words.meaning: adj. 有同情心的，慈悲的
